title: MyCR 全量执行报告 - 2026-07-09 comment-only sweep
overview: 本次先在 mycr 仓库执行 `git pull --ff-only`，结果 already up to date；随后对 trpc-group/trpc-agent-go 做全量 PR 盘点。最终 open PR 为 56 个，checks 快照为 27 个全绿、29 个非绿。本轮严格执行“只发 comments，不做 LGTM、不 approve、不显式合入”的要求，因此没有任何 approval 或 merge 动作。实际新增 COMMENT review 的是 #2149 和 #2047：#2149 是 Code Executor 双语文档，评论聚焦自动执行条件和安全后端示例；#2047 是 durable session context source，评论聚焦可见性判断、multi-message anchor 和持久状态失败处理。#2076 与 #2133 已做深度检查但本轮没有新增阻塞意见，报告在对应 deferred 条目里补充了模块、证据和测试信息。对已有 CHANGES_REQUESTED 的 PR，本次只把最终线程快照中仍有可定位未解决维护者线程的条目放进 changes_requested 分组；没有 unresolved human thread 的 #2044/#2028 改为人工复核待继续处理，避免用 aggregate 状态当作不可审计阻塞。其余 PR 按 own PR、Draft/WIP、CI 非绿、merge conflict、以及 comment-only 下未继续处理的绿色 PR 分组归档，确保每个 open PR 都在报告中出现一次。

commented.title: docs: add sanitized Code Executor blog
commented.author: liuzengh
commented.technical_background: Code Executor lets agents execute model-produced code through configured backends, so its documentation must be precise about automatic execution conditions and safe backend choices.
commented.problem: The project needs a sanitized, bilingual Code Executor article that explains how to configure code execution and response-triggered execution without exposing private endpoints or unsafe defaults.
commented.necessity_assessment: Documentation here is a user-facing execution-safety contract. If readers expect mixed prose plus code to execute, or copy a local backend into a production security path, they can build unsafe or surprising integrations.
commented.evidence_checked: Reviewed the English and Chinese blog additions, MkDocs navigation, the code-execution response processor, current review comments, and final checks.
commented.problem_framing: The PR is documentation-only, but the documented feature can run code. The review therefore treats documentation accuracy and safe backend guidance as part of the user-facing contract.
commented.approach: The article adds a bilingual walkthrough and navigation entries for Code Executor, including setup, automatic response execution, backend selection, and security guidance.
commented.implementation_derivation: The processor accepts exactly one runnable fenced code block after trimming the assistant response, while the blog listed only language and flag conditions. The production/security snippet wires `llmagent.WithCodeExecutor(local.New())`, which conflicts with the later local-backend warning.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The article is useful once the auto-execution contract and backend guidance are aligned with the implementation.
commented.alternative_designs: Either document the exact one-block condition and mark local backend as trusted-only, or switch the security example to a bounded backend such as sandbox/container.
commented.tradeoffs: More precise wording is slightly longer, but prevents readers from treating illustrative local execution as recommended production guidance.
commented.design_assessment: Needs documentation changes before approval; the issues are narrow but touch code execution safety.
commented.api_surface: No exported API changes; user-facing documentation and MkDocs navigation change.
commented.change_inventory: Adds English and Chinese Code Executor blog pages and two navigation entries.
commented.semantic_changes: Documents how to enable Code Executor and response-triggered code execution.
commented.module_impact: Documentation-only change in the mkdocs site.
commented.cross_module_impact: The documented API spans `llmagent`, code executor backends, and the response processor.
commented.behavior_impact: No runtime behavior changes, but inaccurate docs can cause unsafe runtime configurations downstream.
commented.reproduction_on_base: The mismatch is visible by comparing the blog snippets with `internal/flow/processor/codeexecution.go` and its tests for mixed prose, multiple code blocks, and unsupported fences.
commented.issue_evidence_gaps: No product issue context is required; the code path and tests define the behavior.
commented.tests_docs: Ran a URL/internal-text scan on the new blog files; no private endpoint or token pattern was found. CI was green in the collected snapshot.
commented.outcome: Submitted a COMMENT review with two inline documentation comments; no approval, LGTM, or merge action was taken.
commented.risk: The remaining review findings are documentation risks: readers may misconfigure Code Executor or expect automatic execution in cases the processor intentionally skips.
commented.merge_policy: Comment-only run; no LGTM or merge action was allowed.

inline_comments.summary: Document that automatic execution requires the trimmed assistant response to be exactly one runnable fenced code block.
Use a bounded backend or mark `local.New()` as trusted-only in the production/security example, and mirror the Chinese post.

commented.title: agent: add durable session context sources
commented.author: bytethm
commented.technical_background: Durable session context sources let callers persist source-derived context before the current user turn and later decide whether a full snapshot or a diff is needed.
commented.problem: Applications need a durable way to inject source-derived session context before the current user turn and then send only diffs when the prior source context is still visible.
commented.necessity_assessment: This feature controls what context the model sees across turns. A false “snapshot not required” decision can leave the model with stale or partial context while the source callback believes a diff is sufficient.
commented.evidence_checked: Reviewed `runner/session_context.go`, `runner/runner.go`, session filtering behavior, content request filtering, existing CodeRabbit threads, final checks, and local tests for `./agent` and `./runner` plus the append-only diff example.
commented.problem_framing: The PR adds a durable context contract rather than a transient prompt helper, so correctness depends on visibility under the same event filter used for model history and on successful state advancement after context messages are persisted.
commented.approach: The implementation adds `WithSessionContextSource`, records previous state/version and a materialized event anchor, persists context messages before the current turn, and exposes `NeedsSnapshot()` to source callbacks.
commented.implementation_derivation: `SnapshotRequired` is computed from `sessionContextSourceMaterialized(sess, previous)` over the full session, while request construction later applies `ContentRequestProcessor` branch filtering. The commit path anchors each source to `sourceEvents[eventOffset-1]` and logs `UpdateSessionState` failure without returning it.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The feature shape is valuable, but the durable visibility contract needs tighter anchoring, filter-aware checks, and non-silent state persistence failures.
commented.alternative_designs: Track a source event range or first/last anchors, evaluate visibility against the active event-filter scope, and return or atomically handle state persistence errors.
commented.tradeoffs: Range tracking and error propagation add bookkeeping, but avoid silent context loss in long-running or branched sessions.
commented.design_assessment: Needs changes before approval. The comments target durable-state correctness rather than API naming or documentation polish.
commented.api_surface: Adds new runner/agent options for session context sources and documents their use.
commented.change_inventory: Adds source callback types, context message resolution, source state storage, tests, and an append-only diff example.
commented.semantic_changes: A run can now materialize source-provided context into the session transcript and reuse previous source state on later turns.
commented.module_impact: Touches runner session persistence, agent invocation options, content restoration assumptions, docs, and examples.
commented.cross_module_impact: Interacts with session services, summary/trim behavior, content request filtering, and model prompt construction.
commented.behavior_impact: Incorrect source visibility can cause missing snapshots, repeated snapshots, or diffs computed from stale durable state.
commented.reproduction_on_base: The issues are visible from current code: the materialized check scans `sess.GetEvents()` without active filter scope, the anchor is the last source event only, and `UpdateSessionState` errors only log before returning.
commented.issue_evidence_gaps: No external issue context is required; remaining design choice is whether to track first/last anchors or a full range.
commented.tests_docs: Ran `go test ./agent ./runner`, `go test -count=1 ./agent ./runner`, `cd examples && go test -count=1 ./prompt/append_only_diff`, and `cd examples && go run ./prompt/append_only_diff` in the isolated worktree.
commented.outcome: Submitted a COMMENT review with three inline comments; no approval, LGTM, or merge action was taken.
commented.risk: The remaining review findings are durable-context risks: long sessions and branch-filtered histories may silently lose required source context while the callback receives stale assumptions.
commented.merge_policy: Comment-only run and PR currently draft; no LGTM or merge action was allowed.

inline_comments.summary: `SnapshotRequired` should use the same event-filter scope that later builds the model request.
Track a source event range or first source event so multi-message snapshots are not treated as visible when only the last message remains.
Do not silently swallow durable source state persistence failures after context events have been written.

timeline.detail: `git pull --ff-only` reported already up to date.
Open PR count: 56; delta from previous report: new 0, closed 0, changed 0.
Reviewed #2149 and #2047 with posted comments; checked #2076 and #2133 with local validation and no new comments; audited WineChord-owned #2155 and #2156 without self-review action.
Posted COMMENT reviews on #2149 and #2047; no LGTM, approval, or merge action was taken.
Generated a standalone JSON report for archive normalization, HTML rendering, quality checks, and website publication.
A report quality pass found weak changes-requested evidence and thin reviewed-clean entries; the report now includes concrete thread blockers and richer deferred-review details.

follow_up.title: Resolve #2149 documentation blockers
follow_up.reason: The blog should document the exact auto-execution condition and align the security example with a bounded backend.
follow_up.next: Wait for author update, then re-check English and Chinese posts together.

follow_up.title: Resolve #2047 durable context blockers
follow_up.reason: Filter-aware visibility, multi-message anchor tracking, and state persistence failure handling affect model context correctness.
follow_up.next: Ask author to update tests around branch filtering, partial snapshot trimming, and state write failure.

follow_up.title: Own PR maintenance remains separate
follow_up.reason: #2156 has a failing session/pgvector test; this run intentionally did not push to own PR branches.
follow_up.next: Handle CI repair in a dedicated maintenance run where branch writes are explicitly allowed.

skill_evolution.note: Reports should explicitly separate clean-but-deferred PRs from skipped PRs so a comment-only run does not look incomplete.
Design a guarded own-PR maintenance path: isolated worktree, rebase or conflict repair only when writes are allowed, focused tests, and no self-review action.
Generated public report text must avoid internal execution terms and should keep every open PR covered exactly once across processed and skipped sections.
Use explicit GET requests when querying PR comments with gh api pagination so verification cannot accidentally call the review-comment creation endpoint.
Final reports should not use aggregate GitHub CHANGES_REQUESTED as a standalone blocker; include unresolved human thread evidence or classify the PR as needing manual re-audit.
Clean-but-deferred PRs need visible module, evidence, test, and design-summary fields when a run claims they were deeply checked.
